title: Melt In Crispy Argentinian Oxtail Skillet
description: A rich and savory Argentinian dish featuring oxtail, perfect for a date night, with a crispy exterior and a melt in your mouth interior, this recipe is also low sodium friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add the oxtail pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the oxtail from the skillet and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red wine, beef broth, tomato paste, and dried oregano to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Return the oxtail to the skillet and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the oxtail is tender.
Increase the heat to medium high and cook, uncovered, for an additional 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the oxtail is crispy.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley or oregano, if desired.
